inline_comments.message: I have a small problem with that, typical deployment needs helm charts filled. From UX perspective this parameter (if by default disabled) can easily trigger that someone will forget to fill it and create package w/o helm charts. All of that just because we would like to support deployment w/o helm charts. What about by default having it enabled with some "to be filled value" or even keep this file unchanged or just with small update of description.

I modified the path as absolute path is clear as it may suggest to user that certain user or path (absolute path) should be used.

I commented the value out to have the default functionality be Kubernetes cluster without installed application. That is important use case. Will add comment about that if not yet done in further commits.

Also commented out because there cannot be reasonable default value for this (same for all configuration in this file except package base name) and commenting out have a valid default functionality.

Would like to keep handling similar for all parameters whihc cannot have default value (all commented out).
will have better comments etc. in subsequent commit.
aren't we removing this prefix ? but maybe in another commit

I think we have default params for this, OOM helm charts are by default placed according to our build instructions https://gerrit.onap.org/r/gitweb?p=oom/offline-installer.git;a=blob;f=doc/BuildGuide.rst;h=6e36b253267a34773568b998d4ce7fc0a7fcbbfa;hb=HEAD#l299
into ./ansible/application/helm_charts as of now we still need to have OUR (patched) helm charts. this must be well mentioned in description. I don't agree that deployment w/o app is important use case, we never used it.

deployment without app is important use case even if you don't agree. We will need it even by ourselves for testing. And for sure others will require that.

In coming commits we can change the example value here to point to similar location mentioned in guide. And improve the description if not already done in subsequent commits.
What for do we need to comment it out in case the default use case is to have it there? Could we make it easier to edit for user for the default purpose?

Please, check the latest version of this file (the last commit in this chain). All of the variables in this file are commented out. 
As we cannot provide any working default value for any of the variables in this file, this file is just documenting the configuration supported.

In latest version I have changed the example value to follow the BuildGuide example paths (yet real working paths cannot be provided as it always depends on user environment).

We could provide an scripts e.g. "build_onap" and "package_onap" where all paths are really fixed.

this looks to be the only tricky point before accepting/merging all dependent commits. As per our understanding we can't have package.conf with everything commented-out. There must be some way how to get some default values for most of the stuff including HELM_CHARTS_DIR (our default deployment). As a default values we can use values we are using, so it might be simply reusable as it is ?? Isn't it better than "empty" config file with a lot of text and description ? It's ok that user can fill everything, but ideally it should just work out of the box for the default way ...

The idea is same as in ansible. We document available parameters with examples and provide reasonable default values for those we can.
In this case there is no parameter we can give default value so that it would work out of the box. User should anyway generate own config file for the situation at hand, it cannot be run as it is if we provide some absolute paths here for our example.

For accepting these commits this detail should not have anything to do. If it's really up to this, I can hardcode those paths to this file according to our BuildGuide example. Will not do it in this commit, but the last commit in the chain.
Please, check now from this commit: https://gerrit.onap.org/r/#/c/78174/
